comment_text.sn14.13:2.2: The identity of this monk is uncertain. Most manuscripts, and the commentary, simply have Kaccāna here, which is probably not “Kaccāna the Great”. Some manuscripts add a personal name, Saddha or Sandha, while the Chinese parallel at SA 457 has 婆迦利, evidently for Vakkali. He may be the Sabhiya Kaccāna who appears in the same location at SN 44.11.
comment_text.sn14.13:2.4: The problem of false enlightened ones is discussed at DN 29:4.1, DN 33:1.6.5, MN 11:13.6, MN 49:30.9, MN 104:2.5, and SN 55.25:10.4.
comment_text.sn14.13:3.2: The appearance of “person” here anticipates the analysis of different kinds of person in the following discourses through to SN 14.29.

root_text.sn14.13:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā ñātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.sn14.13:2.1: “Dhātuṁ, bhikkhave, paṭicca uppajjati saññā, uppajjati diṭṭhi, uppajjati vitakko”ti.
root_text.sn14.13:2.2: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā kaccāno bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn14.13:2.4: ‘asammāsambuddhesu sammāsambuddhā’ti, ayaṁ nu kho, bhante, diṭṭhi kiṁ paṭicca paññāyatī”ti?
root_text.sn14.13:3.1: “Mahati kho esā, kaccāna, dhātu yadidaṁ avijjādhātu.
root_text.sn14.13:3.2: Hīnaṁ, kaccāna, dhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati hīnā saññā, hīnā diṭṭhi, hīno vitakko, hīnā cetanā, hīnā patthanā, hīno paṇidhi, hīno puggalo, hīnā vācā;
root_text.sn14.13:3.3: hīnaṁ ācikkhati deseti paññapeti paṭṭhapeti vivarati vibhajati uttānīkaroti;
root_text.sn14.13:4.1: Majjhimaṁ, kaccāna, dhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati majjhimā saññā, majjhimā diṭṭhi, majjhimo vitakko, majjhimā cetanā, majjhimā patthanā, majjhimo paṇidhi, majjhimo puggalo, majjhimā vācā;
root_text.sn14.13:4.2: majjhimaṁ ācikkhati deseti paññapeti paṭṭhapeti vivarati vibhajati uttānīkaroti;
root_text.sn14.13:5.1: Paṇītaṁ, kaccāna, dhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati paṇītā saññā, paṇītā diṭṭhi, paṇīto vitakko, paṇītā cetanā, paṇītā patthanā, paṇīto paṇidhi, paṇīto puggalo, paṇītā vācā;
root_text.sn14.13:5.2: paṇītaṁ ācikkhati deseti paññapeti paṭṭhapeti vivarati vibhajati uttānīkaroti;

translation_text.sn14.13:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.sn14.13:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn14.13:2.1: “Mendicants, an element gives rise to a perception, a view, and a thought.”
translation_text.sn14.13:2.2: When he said this, Venerable Kaccāna said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn14.13:2.4: those who are not fully awakened Buddhas, there is a view that they are in fact fully awakened Buddhas. Due to what does this view appear?”
translation_text.sn14.13:3.1: “It’s a mighty thing, Kaccāna, the element of ignorance.
translation_text.sn14.13:3.2: An inferior element gives rise to inferior perception, inferior view, inferior thought, inferior intention, inferior aim, inferior wish, an inferior person, and inferior speech.
translation_text.sn14.13:3.3: One explains, teaches, asserts, establishes, clarifies, analyzes, and reveals the inferior.
translation_text.sn14.13:3.4: I say that their rebirth is inferior.
translation_text.sn14.13:4.1: A middling element gives rise to middling perception, middling view, middling thought, middling intention, middling aim, middling wish, a middling person, and middling speech.
translation_text.sn14.13:4.2: One explains, teaches, asserts, establishes, clarifies, analyzes, and reveals the middling.
translation_text.sn14.13:4.3: I say that their rebirth is middling.
translation_text.sn14.13:5.1: A superior element gives rise to superior perception, superior view, superior thought, superior intention, superior aim, superior wish, a superior person, and superior speech.
translation_text.sn14.13:5.2: One explains, teaches, asserts, establishes, clarifies, analyzes, and reveals the superior.
translation_text.sn14.13:5.3: I say that their rebirth is superior.”

variant_text.sn14.13:2.2: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā kaccāno → saddho kaccāyano (pts1ed, pts2ed); saddho kaccāno (mr)
